_about: Input format templates — GLOBAL, task-agnostic structural knowledge for the top 15 format families covering ~60% of CyberGym tasks. Each entry describes the minimum valid file structure the agent needs to construct before violating a single field at the sink. Level-1 compliant: no CVE data, no patch info, just format specifications any developer knows.

elf.label: ELF (Executable and Linkable Format)
elf.structure: ELF header (64B for 64-bit) → program header table → section header table → sections. e_ident[0..3] = magic. e_ident[4] = class (1=32-bit, 2=64-bit). e_ident[5] = endianness (1=LE, 2=BE).

key_fields.e_shstrndx: offset 62, 2B — section name string table index

postscript.label: PostScript / PDF (Ghostscript)
postscript.structure: PostScript: %!PS-Adobe-N.M header → operators/procedures → showpage. PDF: %PDF-1.X header → body objects → xref table → trailer → %%EOF.

key_fields.ps_operators: moveto, lineto, curveto, fill, stroke, show, image, colorimage

media-container.label: Media containers (AVI/MKV/FLV/WAV/OGG/MVE)
media-container.structure: Container-specific: AVI = RIFF + LIST + chunks. MKV = EBML header + Segment + Clusters. FLV = header(9B) + tags. OGG = pages with sync pattern. MVE = 26-byte signature + chunk stream.

key_fields.avi_riff: RIFF(4B) + size(4B LE) + AVI (4B) — then LIST chunks
key_fields.mkv_ebml: EBML element ID 0x1A45DFA3 + size + EBML version/doctype elements
key_fields.flv_header: FLV(3B) + version(1B) + flags(1B: bit4=audio, bit0=video) + data_offset(4B BE, typically 9)
key_fields.ogg_page: OggS(4B) + version(1B=0) + type(1B) + granule(8B) + serial(4B) + page_seq(4B) + crc(4B) + segments(1B) + segment_table
key_fields.mve_signature: 'Interplay MVE File\x1a\x00' (20B) + '\x1a\x00\x00\x00\x01\x2c' (6B) = 26B total signature. ipmovie_read_header reads into a fixed signature_buffer (typically 21B) via avio_read. CRITICAL for MSan bugs: input SHORTER than the signature_buffer size leaves stack memory uninitialized — even a 1-byte or 0-byte input can trigger use-of-uninitialized-value in memcmp of the signature.
key_fields.mve_chunk: chunk_type(2B LE) + chunk_size(2B LE) + chunk_data. Types: 0x0000=end, 0x0001=init_audio, 0x0002=audio_only, 0x0003=init_video, 0x0004=video, 0x0005=shutdown

asn1-smartcard.label: ASN.1 / Smart card (PKCS#15, ISO 7816, APDU simulation)
asn1-smartcard.magic: none (TLV-encoded, starts with a tag byte)
asn1-smartcard.structure: TLV (Tag-Length-Value) encoding. Tag: 1-3 bytes (class + constructed + number). Length: 1-5 bytes (short form <128, long form 0x8N + N bytes). Value: Length bytes of content. Nested for SEQUENCE/SET.

key_fields.tag_byte: bits 7-6: class (00=universal, 01=application, 10=context, 11=private). bit 5: constructed(1)/primitive(0). bits 4-0: tag number (0x1F = long form follows)
key_fields.length: byte < 0x80: short form (length = byte). byte = 0x80: indefinite. byte = 0x81-0x84: long form, next 1-4 bytes = length
key_fields.apdu_simulation: OpenSC fuzz_card uses a virtual reader (fuzzer_reader.c) that simulates APDU communication via a chunk-based format. Input layout: [flags: 8B (unsigned long)] + [len: 1B (uint8_t)] + [reader_data...]. Reader data is a sequence of CHUNKS: each chunk = [chunk_size: 2B LE] + [chunk_data: chunk_size bytes]. The FIRST chunk is the ATR (Answer To Reset — selects the card driver). Subsequent chunks are APDU RESPONSES consumed by sc_transmit_apdu: last 2 bytes of each chunk = SW1 + SW2 (status words), preceding bytes = response data. SW1=0x90 SW2=0x00 means success. Bug pattern: driver checks SW1==0x90 but not SW2, or proceeds with short/empty response data.
key_fields.apdu_sw_bytes: SW1=0x90: success class (SW2=0x00 is OK, others may indicate warnings). SW1=0x61: more data available (SW2=remaining bytes). SW1=0x6X: error class. Common bug: code checks only SW1==0x90 without verifying SW2==0x00, proceeding with uninitialized/unallocated data.

pcap-network.label: PCAP / Network protocols
pcap-network.magic: a1b2c3d4 (PCAP LE) or d4c3b2a1 (PCAP BE) or 0a0d0d0a (PCAPNG)
pcap-network.structure: PCAP: global header (24B) → packet records (header 16B + data). PCAPNG: Section Header Block → Interface Description Block → Enhanced Packet Blocks.

key_fields.pcap_global: magic(4B) + version_major(2B=2) + version_minor(2B=4) + thiszone(4B=0) + sigfigs(4B=0) + snaplen(4B) + network(4B: 1=Ethernet, 101=raw IP)
key_fields.pcap_packet: ts_sec(4B) + ts_usec(4B) + incl_len(4B) + orig_len(4B) + data(incl_len bytes)
key_fields.pcapng_shb: block_type=0x0A0D0D0A + block_len(4B) + BOM(4B=0x1A2B3C4D) + version(2+2B) + section_length(8B=-1) + options + block_len(4B)

raw-image.label: Camera RAW images (CR2, NEF, ARW, DNG)
raw-image.magic: varies: TIFF-based (49492a00 LE or 4d4d002a BE), DNG has TIFF magic + DNG tags
raw-image.structure: TIFF IFD chain: header (8B: byte_order + magic 42 + IFD0_offset) → IFD0 (count + entries + next_IFD_offset) → SubIFDs → strip/tile data. RAW-specific tags in maker notes.

ruby-source.label: Ruby source / mruby bytecode
ruby-source.magic: none for source; 5249544500 (RITE) for mruby bytecode (.mrb)
ruby-source.structure: Source: UTF-8 text, Ruby syntax. Bytecode (RITE): RITE header (22B) → IREP section → LINE section → END section.

key_fields.rite_crc: 2 bytes, CRC of the binary section
key_fields.irep_section: IREP tag(4B) + size(4B) + version(4B) + instruction records

xml.label: XML / HTML / SVG
xml.magic: 3c3f786d6c (<?xml) for XML declaration, 3c21444f43 (<!DOC) for DOCTYPE, or bare <root>
xml.structure: <?xml version="1.0"?> (optional) → <!DOCTYPE ...> (optional) → root element → child elements. Well-formed: every open tag has a close tag, attributes quoted, entities escaped.

font.label: TrueType / OpenType fonts (TTF/OTF/WOFF/CFF2)
font.structure: Offset table (12B): sfVersion + numTables + searchRange + entrySelector + rangeShift → table directory (16B per table: tag + checksum + offset + length) → table data. NOTE: FreeType's ftfuzzer accepts input as an uncompressed tar archive (via libarchive) — first entry is the font, additional entries are attached streams. Raw (non-tar) input also works as a single font.

key_fields.sfVersion: offset 0, 4B — 0x00010000 for TTF, 'OTTO' for CFF-based OTF
key_fields.required_tables: cmap (char mapping), head (font header), hhea (horizontal header), hmtx (horizontal metrics), maxp (max profile), name (naming), OS/2 (OS metrics), post (PostScript)
key_fields.cff2_table: CFF2 table (tag 'CFF2') for OpenType variable fonts. Header: major(1B=2) + minor(1B) + headerSize(1B) + topDictLength(2B). Top DICT → Font DICT → Private DICT → CharStrings. CFF2 supports variation via 'blend' operator (opcode 23) and ItemVariationStore (vsindex + blend). IMPORTANT: multiple consecutive blend operators cause blend_stack reallocation — if parser->stack pointers into blend_stack are not updated after realloc, they become stale (UAF). This is a known bug pattern in CFF2 parsers.
key_fields.variable_font: Variable fonts use 'fvar' table (axes), 'gvar' (glyph variations), 'CFF2' (CFF2 variations via blend). FreeType's setIntermediateAxis selects midpoint coordinates. Crafting: include fvar with at least 1 axis + CFF2 with blend operators to exercise the variation path.

pdf.label: PDF (Portable Document Format)
pdf.structure:
%PDF-1.X header → body (indirect objects: N 0 obj...endobj) → cross-reference table (xref 0 N
<entries>) → trailer (trailer<<...>> startxref OFFSET %%EOF). Streams: stream
<data>
endstream.

key_fields.xref:
xref
0 N
OOOOOOOOOO GGGGG f/n 
 (10-digit offset, 5-digit generation, f=free/n=in-use)
key_fields.page: /Type /Page /Parent P 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 W H] /Contents C 0 R

network-dpi.label: Network DPI payloads
network-dpi.structure: Protocol-specific: HTTP = request line + headers + body. DNS = header(12B) + questions + answers. TLS = record(5B) + handshake. The harness typically wraps raw protocol data in a PCAP or passes it directly.

key_fields.dns_header: id(2B) + flags(2B) + qdcount(2B) + ancount(2B) + nscount(2B) + arcount(2B)

dwg.label: DWG / DXF (AutoCAD)
dwg.structure: DWG: version string (6B, e.g. 'AC1032') → header section → class section → object map → objects → R2004+ uses compressed sections. DXF: ASCII text with SECTION/ENDSEC pairs (HEADER, CLASSES, TABLES, BLOCKS, ENTITIES, OBJECTS).

key_fields.dwg_header_vars: INSBASE, EXTMIN, EXTMAX, LIMMIN, LIMMAX, etc.
key_fields.entity_types: LINE, CIRCLE, ARC, TEXT, INSERT, POLYLINE, LWPOLYLINE, DIMENSION

raster-image.label: Raster images (TIFF/PNG/JPEG/GIF/BMP/WebP)
raster-image.structure: PNG: signature(8B) + IHDR chunk + data chunks (IDAT) + IEND. JPEG: SOI(FFD8) + markers (FFxx + length + data) + SOS + scan data + EOI(FFD9). TIFF: header(8B) + IFD chain. GIF: header(6B) + LSD(7B) + GCT + blocks. BMP: header(14B) + DIB header + pixel data.

key_fields.png_ihdr: width(4B BE) + height(4B BE) + bit_depth(1B) + color_type(1B) + compression(1B=0) + filter(1B=0) + interlace(1B)
key_fields.png_chunk: length(4B BE) + type(4B ASCII) + data(length B) + CRC(4B)
key_fields.jpeg_sof: FFCx marker: precision(1B) + height(2B BE) + width(2B BE) + components(1B) + component_specs

mp4-isobmff.label: MP4 / ISOBMFF / HEIF (ISO Base Media File Format)
mp4-isobmff.magic: varies by brand: 66747970 ('ftyp' box at offset 4); first 4 bytes = box size
mp4-isobmff.structure: Sequence of boxes: size(4B BE) + type(4B ASCII) + data. Key boxes: ftyp (file type) → moov (movie metadata) → mdat (media data). moov contains mvhd + trak(s). Each trak has tkhd + mdia (mdhd + hdlr + minf + stbl). HEIF: ftyp(heic/mif1) → meta (hdlr+pitm+iloc+iprp+iref) → mdat.

key_fields.box: size(4B BE, 0=extends to EOF, 1=64-bit extended size follows) + type(4B ASCII)
key_fields.ftyp: major_brand(4B) + minor_version(4B) + compatible_brands(4B each). HEIF brands: 'heic' (HEVC), 'mif1' (generic HEIF)
key_fields.mvhd: version(1B) + flags(3B) + creation_time + modification_time + timescale(4B) + duration
key_fields.tkhd: version(1B) + flags(3B) + track_ID(4B) + duration + width(4B fixed-point) + height(4B)
key_fields.stbl_tables: stts (time-to-sample), stsc (sample-to-chunk), stsz (sample sizes), stco/co64 (chunk offsets)
key_fields.heif_meta: meta box contains: hdlr (handler='pict'), pitm (primary item ID), iloc (item locations → offsets into mdat), iprp (item properties: ispe=image size, hvcC=HEVC config, auxC=aux type). iref box links items (e.g. auxl=auxiliary image link, thmb=thumbnail).
key_fields.heif_alpha: Alpha auxiliary image: auxC box with type 'urn:mpeg:hevc:2015:auxid:1'. When decoding, libheif creates a separate alpha plane. Bug pattern: code calls copy_new_plane_from(src, heif_channel_Alpha, heif_channel_Y) but if the alpha channel doesn't exist, get_width/get_height return -1 → crash in allocation/copy.
key_fields.heif_color_conversion_fuzzer: libheif also has a color_conversion_fuzzer that takes RAW BYTES (not HEIF files): [width:2B] [height:2B] [bit_depth:1B] [alpha_flag:1B] [in_chroma:1B] [in_colorspace:1B] [out_chroma:1B] [out_colorspace:1B] + plane pixel data. Width/height must be even and non-zero. This harness directly tests convert_colorspace() without HEIF parsing. NOTE: CyberGym may use file-fuzzer (AFL, reads actual HEIF files) instead of color_conversion_fuzzer — check the submit.sh binary path.

3d-model.label: 3D model formats (MD3/MD2/MDL/OBJ/STL/PLY)
3d-model.structure: MD3: header(108B) → frames(NUM_FRAMES * 56B) → tags(NUM_FRAMES * NUM_TAGS * 112B) → surfaces(variable). Each surface has its own header + shaders + triangles + texcoords + vertices.

key_fields.md3_header: magic('IDP3', 4B) + version(4B LE, must be 15) + name(64B) + flags(4B) + NUM_FRAMES(4B) + NUM_TAGS(4B) + NUM_SURFACES(4B) + NUM_SKINS(4B) + OFS_FRAMES(4B) + OFS_TAGS(4B) + OFS_SURFACES(4B) + OFS_EOF(4B)
key_fields.md3_surface: magic('IDP3', 4B) + name(64B) + flags(4B) + NUM_FRAMES(4B) + NUM_SHADERS(4B) + NUM_VERTICES(4B) + NUM_TRIANGLES(4B) + OFS_TRIANGLES(4B) + OFS_SHADERS(4B) + OFS_TEXCOORDS(4B) + OFS_XYZNORMALS(4B) + OFS_END(4B)
key_fields.md3_validation: assimp ValidateHeaderOffsets checks: OFS_FRAMES + NUM_FRAMES*56 <= fileSize, OFS_TAGS + NUM_TAGS*NUM_FRAMES*112 <= fileSize, OFS_SURFACES + NUM_SURFACES*sizeof(Surface) <= fileSize. ValidateSurfaceHeaderOffsets checks per-surface offsets. IMPORTANT: validation checks header-level counts but may not fully validate surface DATA sizes (triangles*12B, vertices*8B per frame) — the gap between validated header offsets and actual data consumption is where overflow can occur.

dwarf-debug.label: DWARF debug info / Compression formats
dwarf-debug.magic: DWARF: embedded in ELF .debug_* sections. Blosc2: 0x0210 (Blosc2 frame magic) or varies.
dwarf-debug.structure: DWARF: .debug_info (compilation units with DIE trees), .debug_abbrev (abbreviation tables), .debug_line (line number program), .debug_str (string table). Blosc2: header (meta + nchunks + frame sections) → compressed chunks.

key_fields.dwarf_cu_header: unit_length(4B) + version(2B: 4 or 5) + debug_abbrev_offset(4B) + address_size(1B: 4 or 8)
key_fields.dwarf_leb128: variable-length encoding: 7 bits per byte, high bit = more bytes follow
key_fields.blosc2_header: magic(2B) + version(1B) + flags(1B) + typesize(4B) + nbytes(8B) + cbytes(8B) + blocksize(4B)
key_fields.blosc2_chunk: magic(1B) + flags(1B) + typesize(1B) + nbytes(4B) + blocksize(4B) + cbytes(4B) + data
